tool.name: ALNchain
tool.description: filter .1aln alignments into one-to-one global chains

param.name: input
param.label: Input .1aln alignment file
param.help: A .1aln file produced by FastGA. In the FastGA tool, select the '1aln (-1)' output format to generate one.

param.name: max_gap
param.label: Maximum gap between consecutive alignments (bp)
param.help: Upper bound on the gap allowed between two consecutive alignments in a chain. Larger values join more distant alignments into a single chain.

param.name: max_overlap
param.label: Maximum overlap between consecutive alignments (bp)
param.help: Upper bound on how much two consecutive alignments in a chain may overlap.

param.name: gap_penalty
param.label: Linear gap penalty coefficient
param.help: Per-base penalty applied to gaps when scoring a chain.

param.name: overlap_penalty
param.label: Linear overlap penalty coefficient
param.help: Per-base penalty applied to overlaps when scoring a chain.

param.name: score_drop
param.label: Score drop threshold for chain termination
param.help: A chain is terminated if its running score drops by more than this value.

param.name: close_gap_limit
param.label: Upper limit for closing gaps within a chain (bp)
param.help: Gaps smaller than this are closed when emitting a chain.

param.name: min_chain_size
param.label: Minimum chain size (bp)
param.help: Chains shorter than this in the target sequence are discarded.

param.name: min_chain_count
param.label: Minimum number of alignments per chain
param.help: Chains containing fewer alignments than this are discarded.

param.name: chain_coverage
param.label: Minimum chain coverage (fraction)
param.help: Fraction of the chain's span that must be covered by its alignments for the chain to be retained.

param.name: seq_coverage
param.label: Minimum sequence coverage (fraction)
param.help: Fraction of new sequence a chain must contribute relative to already-selected chains to be retained. 0 disables the filter.

tool.help:
ALNchain post-processes a ``.1aln`` alignment file produced by FastGA to produce a one-to-one global alignment (allowing rearrangements) by selecting the best-scored local chains of alignments.
A *chain* is a sequence of collinear alignments between two contigs whose consecutive members are within allowed gap and overlap tolerances. Each chain is assigned a score of the form ``C - G*p - O*q`` where ``C`` is the number of unique positions covered, ``G`` is total gap, and ``O`` is total overlap. Chains are then ranked by score and greedily selected while they contribute enough new coverage.
-----
Input *****
A single ``.1aln`` dataset (for example the ``1aln`` output of the FastGA tool). The ``.1aln`` format is the compact ONEcode binary produced by FastGA.
-----
Options *******
**Chain construction**
=================================== ======== =========== **Option** **Flag** **Default** ----------------------------------- -------- ----------- Maximum gap between alignments -g 10000 Maximum overlap between alignments -l 10000 Linear gap penalty coefficient -p 0.1 Linear overlap penalty coefficient -q 0.1 Score drop termination threshold -z 1000 Upper limit for closing gaps -f 1000 =================================== ======== ===========
**Chain filtering**
==================================== ======== =========== **Option** **Flag** **Default** ------------------------------------ -------- ----------- Minimum chain size (bp) -s 10000 Minimum alignments per chain -n 1 Minimum chain coverage (fraction) -c 0.5 Minimum sequence coverage (fraction) -e 0.0 ==================================== ======== ===========
-----
Output ******
A filtered ``.1aln`` file (delivered as Galaxy ``txt`` because no ``1aln`` datatype is registered) containing only the selected chains. On small or simple inputs where every alignment already satisfies the defaults, the output may look nearly identical to the input; ALNchain only appends a provenance line to the file header in that case.
